transcript.text: So, let me turn up my volume here and connect my AirPods.
Can you hear me?
Yep.
Can you hear me?
Perfectly.
All right.
Very nice to meet you.
Thanks for jumping on quickly.
Yeah, no worries.
I figured this was easier and I couldn't be bothered to type up a bunch of stuff today.
I think sometimes just talking these through is better than email back and forth.
Yeah.
I think we can just go through each one.
Some are totally fine and then a few I want to talk about.
The first is for the term for convenience.
So, we don't have this for any of our clients.
Okay.
the main reason being is that-
It would be, like, very hard for us to build a VC fundable company if we had cancel at any time abilities.
And because we're building a platform on the side, right, we're, like, hiring engineers and everything.
So all of our agreements are 12 months and have, like, if you want, I can add something that's something about, like, termination for breach or something like this.

Yeah, listen, I think what it comes down to is when you look through the statement of work, and if you consider the statement of work an agreement, which it is, then, you know, essentially how it reads is after phase one, which I see in here, the text I sent back that I had my lawyer quickly review here was, you know, might make it seem like we're going to cancel phase one.
That's not the goal at all, right?
So it's basically once we enter phase two, it's 12 months.
It's guaranteed.

And listen, I have no doubt that we'll keep working with you guys for 12 months, but it's one of these where if suddenly, you know, we feel like things aren't working out, if it's not delivering what we expect it's delivering, if we're not seeing, you know, the volume of content being created that we expected, the contract right now, basically, we can't exit it, right?
And so that's the problem.
And so if you have some suggested breach language, if you have some more, you know, language around what is expected and guaranteed and will constitute breach, I'm totally open for that.
It's just, you know, this is this termination for convenience, I think is a legal lawyer's term that they love to have.
And I'm not a lawyer, I'll say that.
So I just have someone I go to for
For assistance.
But, you know, so it's not really, like, convenience is not what I'm going for.
It's really more just, it's not as if willy-nilly we're saying, hey, we don't want to use it anymore.

It really is more of a, if this is truly not delivering the content that we expected, or it's not of a high enough quality, or, you know, there's issues with it, then we want to have a remedy to that.
Yeah.
Or the ability to cancel out the contract.

Yeah, I think we can do something about, like, if we're, because there is even, like, a set number of amount of content and everything we have to deliver, we can put in something there, like, if we don't do that, it's breach.

And then the one I normally use is, like, if we're in breach, there's, like, a notice period to correct the breach, which you sort of had enough one further down about, maybe it was similar to the KPI thing, which is, like, if we're not doing the work, like, I mean, I would even, we could even shorten that to, like, like, 30 days in the morning, and then that's it kind of thing to correct it.
Like, like,

But the reason, like, the other reason we don't do any kind of, like, term for convenience is, and it's why we have the phase one, is so that, like, we do two months of work together, and then if you don't want to keep working together, that's totally fine, or we don't want to work with you, or whatever, for whatever reason, like, that's, we've done, like, a full trial period together, and at that point, we're, like, in agreement that we're going to go together for the next 12 months.

The other thing we can do, yeah, the other thing we can do, just to make it easy, then, is the contract today is written so that, unless we specifically give notice at the end of phase one, it automatically goes into phase two, and we're locked into 12 months, if we want to push the ball a little bit further down the road, let's, you know, add whatever breach language you're comfortable with.
Maybe we just remove the, you know, remove the automatically moves into phase two language, and then, you know, if that gets to what you guys are looking for.
And then, you know, assuming everything's great, then it's a quick little addendum saying, yes, we're moving to phase two.
So what I can do, and I just did this, was make it like opt-in, was like, you have...
And that's what I'm getting at, which is like, right now it's a, if I forget to send a message or whatever, you know, then automatically goes in, we're locked in for 12 months.
So, so yeah, let's, let's, let's, let's do the whole opt-in and then...
And we'll do something about breach.
Yeah.

And so one of the things I noticed is there's actually no service level or KPI, you know, I guess there's description of the work, but there's nothing that really is KPIs or service level agreements in the scope of work, at least how, how, how we read it.
And so that's why three really goes with that.
think three can, three can help with the language around, around breach of...
Yeah, and what, like, what are you thinking there?
We would determine KPIs at some point and then, or what do you think?
Yeah, and maybe this is more of a phase two thing, right?
So specifically, I mean, really what I'm more concerned about is once, again, I expect that the first two months and the, or however long that phase one works to go well.
I have no doubt on that.

It's just more of a concern of great things have gone well and then moving to phase two and then suddenly everything falls off the radar, which, again, you guys are growing company, we're growing company.
I don't think anyone has any intention of that.
But let's say you guys can't get the next round of funding.
Let's say you suddenly have to lay off half your staff and now you can't deliver on those promises.
And, you know, and here we are in a 12-month contract that we can't get out of and you guys aren't delivering.
That's kind of the untenable part for me.
And likewise, when I go raise our next round, Series A.
Then, you know, in the clean room, they're going to look at the contracts.
They're going to look at this and say, okay, you guys have signed up for this.
You're not getting, you know, the remedies or you're not getting the deliverables.
You know, we've got a bunch of spend here.
That's not very wise.
Yeah, yeah, that's fair.
far as that's not.
So it's kind of a, I understand you guys need the revenue, but.
The other way it's written is we can very easily do some kind of like amendment after phase one.
If like you feel, if we've realigned on some different scope in phase one, that's the point of it.
It's like, oh, we actually want to do these different work streams.
Like actually like plot twist.
We want to change completely.
We can, we'll just like, we'll put that into paper.
So then it's no longer like the standard.
think a lot of the issues here, and I'm surprised if you guys haven't run into this before.
think a lot of the issues here is that phase two is automatic unless you opt out.
And, and therefore a lot more focus on the initial contract is going to be on phase two.
The, the.
Main reason we do that, a lot of our clients are like public companies where procurement is terrible.
And so it's like, for their sake as well, I don't want to have to pause at week seven and we go into a five-week procurement cycle.
So we've been finding it easier to just get the whole thing through procurement.
And then like, yes, they have this out if they need to, and it's fine.
And so that's the main reason for it.
Makes sense.
I can get on the phone and, you know, obviously, and turn things around in a day and a half, two days.
Yeah, exactly.
A bit different.
Okay.
So I feel like those three together probably cover.
And then the logo stuff, that's fine.
So you just, okay.
So you just written consent.
Yeah, that's totally fine.
Yeah.
I mean, and it's just more of a, you know, and I think how we wrote it here is that it's kind of both sides.
Oh, no, we're, we're, it's not on our side, but it's just, it's just, it's just, it's just more of a, it's just more of a, you know, let's, let's at least control where our mark goes.
Yeah.
And frankly, if things are going well, then I'd love to have a logo on your website anyway, because it creates more buzz about us as well.
Yeah.
Indemnification is totally fine.
We can add that.
Governing law of Delaware is good.
We're a Delaware biz.
The venue of Washington is not ideal, but we can keep that.
Yeah, so we're in Seattle, and so what basically was recommended to me was to essentially say, yes, it's Delaware, as long as it's, you know, without regard to any conflict of laws.
And then it basically says, if you guys are disputing anything, then you guys get to pick where it is.
If we're disputing anything, then we get to pick is basically how I believe it's written.
Should we go to the student panel in last day?
I think both in all situations, it's going to Washington.
Um, I won't die on that, the hill of that one.
Okay.
And again, if you guys have anyone and you want to rewrite that to be one of the two, that's just, that's fine with me as well.
Okay.
Um, so then tell me about the, I haven't signed a PIAA for an, um, for a customer before, but we can definitely sign an NDA.
I can, I can send you our PIAA, which basically is an NDA and privacy and inventions assignment, uh, combined.
We have a specific one.
So we use Perkins Cooey as our main lawyer here in Seattle.
Uh, they do like 99% of all the startups and, and, and, you know, venture funded companies and they have a standard, uh, vendor PIAA that, that they recommend that we execute.
I can send that across.
I recognize I haven't, uh, I can send that across.
Let me take a look.
straightforward.
Okay.

NDAs prefer, just because we use the PIIA for employees, for example, who like, if they don't disclose they invented something and then they work for us and they, whatever, we think it's ours, they think it's theirs kind of thing.
So I don't know that it's as relevant because there's already some work product language in that.
I'll send it across to you.
I'll send it across to you and take look.
So we have a specific vendor one.
And so it's just more of a, if the vendor creates things, then it becomes ours.
I'm pretty, yeah.
I mean, let me, I will look because there's already work, there's already work product language in SSL.
So let's see if it's redundant, basically.
Okay.
And then, okay, billing.
So we bill, we can do monthly, but we do bill upfront because with net 30, you're going to end up paying us after we've.
We've already done an entire month's work.
So for us, for like, because we are service heavy, right?
We have like staff working on the accounts.
It would be like quite problematic if we were effectively net 60, if you want us to do in arrears plus and net 30.
I understand the principle.
I've worked in consulting.
I've worked in service businesses for, you know, much of my early career.
I've never, frankly, heard of any service or vendor agreement where it's paid up front other than software, you know, maintenance fees, which tend to be paid up front.
But typically, anything that is create work product is create work product and then pay for work product, not pay up front for the promise of work product.
And so that's why I'd love to understand how this is different.
Well, with net 30, you're not really paying up front.
front.
If you would pay, most people pay on day 30, right?
Yeah, so it's not so much the date of the payment.
It's more acceptance of deliverables and payment, right?
So, you know, at the end of the day, money is the ultimate arbiter of, you know, being able to withhold funds to get remedies.
And so if funds have already been paid, then there's very little remedies that we can get from that standpoint, other than going and pursuing some legal, which I don't want to get into.
And that's where I just, you know, again, I'm happy for you guys, but I'm surprised that you are having folks pay up front.
And we actually don't have any.
We have probably five people on monthly.
We usually do quarterly up front.
Let me take this one back and think about it.
Okay.
I don't want any of this stuff to be- the way, if something were to go sideways, you can trust we would do a refund or something.
There's no situation in which we'd be withholding your money for something going awry.
For me, it's more about keeping everything standard over here.
And again, monthly for us is we are under the same cash crunch.
I'm sure you guys are as Yeah, it's fine.
It's fine for first earlier stage companies.
Yeah, monthly is fine.
And I think that's it.
Yeah, so basically, I'm going to do an opt-in to phase two.
I'm going to add a breach clause.
Indemnation is good.
Governing law is okay.
You're going to send me the PIA.
You'll get back to me on the billing.
And then just so I don't mess up this KPI thing, I guess I'm not sure looking just for
For more of a, if you guys can, if you can think of any quality of deliverable quality of product metrics.
Yeah, I wonder if we could write like accepted pieces or something like that.
Yeah, right.
and listen, this is, you know, contracts, the only point of contracts is when things go terribly wrong.
Yeah, I know.
everybody would have handshake agreements.
Yeah.
And so, none of this is anything anybody ever expects to go look at every clause.
Again, what I'm looking for more, and we're looking for here more is if, I'll just give the absolute worst case scenario.
Yeah.
If what we're getting is, you know, really well written, it's on point, you know, the math.
The has been listened to, the messaging we've talked about, you know, it's the first phase one goes amazing, you know, all the articles and all the Q&As and the FAQs, everything is amazing.
And then we enter, and then month six, you know, we're starting to get 150 word things that, you know, clearly are now subpar.
Just , yeah.
Yeah, I get it.
Like, like, like, how, like, how do we make, how do we put somewhere that it's not?

Like, there's something in here that says, like, okay, maybe I can just clear it up, clear it up a little bit, the language, but it's like, there's a definition of done here, which is like, approved voice, approved style, passes the SEO checklist, has a visual asset, approved by our managing editor.
Plus the content definition of done.
Yeah, so if there's something we can add there, maybe that's like approved by client or something, then it's like, if I add the term, if I add the.
Yeah, I think it's just more, yeah, so that would work.
It's just the definition of done as defined by who.
So if you can think of some language to put in there or just a couple of words by client, that should cover it as well.
Yeah, and that's like the real goal of the sprint is what is definition of done when we leave the eight weeks kind of thing.
It's like that's the main question we're asking is did we all figure out how to work together basically to get to the point where we can be a bit more autonomous, right, and then keep going.
So, okay, this sounds good.
And then I'm looking for the vendor document right now, but go ahead.
I'll send it to you right after this call.
No, it's just looking at what date you guys are thinking of starting.
So we talked to Tyler and, you know, I think I talked to Marcel two months ago, but, you know, then we...
Sat on it.
Tyler asked the same question.
I said, well, we can start on Monday, which he kind of laughed at.
Labor Day.
You know, I was kind of joking.
I'm like, the whole point is there's nothing holding us back from starting.
Okay.
So it's kind of as soon as makes sense for both sides.
Okay.
I feel like we'll get this wrapped up.
I need to head to a ferry soon, but I'll try to get this wrapped up tomorrow.
And then I'm assuming we'll, we'll get it done on Tuesday, basically.
Perfect.
So then maybe, I think Tyler, he just went on, he's on his bachelor party right now.
So I think he'll be.
Awesome.
He said he was heading to Oregon or something like that.
I know.
I was, I was expecting like some party answer and he said he's going to Mount Hood, like around there.
Yeah.
I, I went to Vegas, I think quite different.
Extremes.
That's how you meet both sides of friends.
We, we, we didn't go wild, but we still, you know, went gambling and.
And did, you know, stuff like that.
Well, I went to Palm Springs and there's a bar there.
I'm totally blanking on the name.
There's like one main bar there, but it's like, you know, the demographics is quite a bit older, as you can imagine, in Palm Springs.
It was so fun.
I think it closed at 11.
We did a huge group of adults.
I think there's probably 30 of us that kind of semi-took over a hotel down Palm Springs a couple years back and had a blast and went out to different bars that, again, there's a huge LMBTQ community.
Oh, yeah, yeah, in downtown?
Yeah.
In downtown.
So we were staying kind of close to downtown.
So, yeah, Palm Springs is a fun place.
Very art deco and you feel like you're in different worlds.
But you can also chill.
It was like, we did a hike.
We laid at the pool.
So you're over in Marin, I take it?
No, I live in Vancouver in Canada.
Okay, okay, so you're up north.
There you go.
Yeah.
Yeah.
We're not, we're not, we're not, I'm not far away in Seattle.
We, we actually have a place up in Whistler.
So, oh, awesome.
So, so, so I, I, I cruised through, uh, cruised through Vancouver quite a, quite a bit.
Are you a skier or a mountain biker?
Skier.
My son loves to mountain bike as well.
So we'll head up a bunch in the summer, but, uh, but it's mainly during the winter.
Nice.
That's awesome.
I actually didn't go once this past season.
didn't get a pass.
So then I was like, oh, day rates.
It's like, and then you look and it's like three, 320 Canadian or something crazy.
I know.
So I started getting into backcountry skiing.
Cause it's like closer.
There's lots in Squamish and then I only have to go halfway and I can just go for a few hours.
And what, even my, even my daughter, who's not a big skier.
I always just get her the, uh, cause we qualify for the edge pass as well.
The, um, BC one.
Um, I'm like, I'm just going to get you a three day.
Yeah.
Because you can always use three days.
And then with a three day, you can add on at 50%.
So.
So even that just kind of means that, no, so it's kind of just locks you into something.
Yeah.
Anybody who skis, especially in Vancouver, you can always do three days of Whistler.
Oh, yeah.
And so that just lets you have a couple extra days if you want to.
Especially if you can hit weekdays, it's like a dream.
Yeah, we avoid it over between kind of Christmas and the 5th of January.
I'll avoid it up there like a plague.
And then Family Weekend, which is also President's Day for us, is also not a great time to head up there.
But yeah, the rest of the time we go up.
Nice.
Very cool.
Well, it's very nice to meet you.
I'll find that NDA-ish PIA and I'll double check it and then send it across.
Cool.
Thank you.
All right.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss and resolve contract terms for a partnership between ottotheagent.com and growthx.ai](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Agreement to modify contract terms: opt-in for Phase 2, add breach clause, adjust KPIs](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=28.0)
  - [Billing terms to be finalized (monthly vs. upfront payment)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=708.0)
  - [PIAA (or NDA) to be reviewed and potentially signed](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
  - [Tentative start date: Tuesday after Labor Day (pending contract finalization)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=1074.0)

## Topics

### Contract Modifications

  - [Shift to opt-in model for Phase 2 instead of automatic renewal](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=264.0)
  - [Add breach clause with 30-day correction period](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=208.0)
  - [Include KPIs or quality metrics in the contract (e.g., "approved by client" in definition of done)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [Logo usage requires written consent from both parties](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=520.0)
  - [Indemnification clause to be added](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Governing law: Delaware, venue: Washington (potential for adjustment)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)

### Billing Terms

  - [Current proposal: Monthly billing, paid upfront](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=708.0)
  - [Michael prefers payment after work completion](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
  - [Bridget to reconsider, citing cash flow concerns for early-stage companies](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=778.0)

### PIAA vs. NDA

  - [Michael to send vendor-specific PIAA for review](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
  - [Bridget prefers standard NDA, will review PIAA to check for redundancy with existing work product language](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=663.0)

### Project Timeline

  - [Proposed start date: Tuesday after Labor Day (September 5, 2025)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=1074.0)
  - [Contract finalization targeted for the following day (August 29, 2025)](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=1098.0)

### Quality Assurance

  - [Discussion on including metrics for deliverable quality](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [Suggestion to add "approved by client" to the definition of done](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=993.0)
  - [Focus on maintaining quality throughout the contract duration](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Bridget to update contract with opt-in for Phase 2 and breach clause](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0)
  - [Michael to send vendor PIAA document for review](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
  - [Michael to finalize decision on billing terms](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=832.0)
  - [Bridget to refine KPI/quality metric language in the contract](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
  - [Both parties to review and finalize contract by August 29, 2025](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=1098.0)
  - [Tentative project start date set for September 5, 2025](https://fathom.video/share/PjujnV3KzmZaQrbwATt5czbCpFjSach3?tab=summary&timestamp=1074.0)

action_items.description: Send PIAA doc to Bridget. Review/decide on billing terms, respond to Bridget.
Update contract - add opt-in for phase 2, breach clause, indemnification, update governing law, add KPI/quality metrics ("approved by client" in def of done). Finalize & send to Michael.
